---
title: "Post Title"
date: "YYYY-MM-DD"
categories: [economics, data visualization]
draft: true
toc: true
toc-location: right
code-fold: true
code-summary: "Show code"
---

<!-- Lead with the key finding or framing question. No Introduction heading. -->

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Reproducing this analysis

The Python code used to generate each chart is included in this post. Click on any
code block to see the full implementation. The complete data pipeline includes:

- `01_fetch_data.py` - Fetches raw data from [source]
- `02_clean_data.py` - Cleans and computes derived series
- `04_compute_stats.py` - Computes summary statistics

All data sourced from [Source Name](https://example.com).
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false

import json
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as ticker
from IPython.display import HTML

POST_DIR = Path(".").resolve()
DATA_DIR = POST_DIR / "data"
STATS_PATH = POST_DIR / "stats" / "summary_stats.json"

with open(STATS_PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
    stats = json.load(f)

COLORS = {
    "primary": "#2e5984",
    "accent": "#8b0000",
    "secondary": "#6b8e6b",
    "neutral": "#4a4a4a",
    "light": "#b0b0b0",
    "fed_target": "#cc0000",
}

plt.rcParams.update({
    "font.family": "serif",
    "font.size": 10,
    "axes.spines.top": False,
    "axes.spines.right": False,
    "axes.grid": False,
    "figure.facecolor": "white",
    "axes.facecolor": "#fafafa",
    "axes.linewidth": 0.5,
})

def fmt(n):
    if isinstance(n, float):
        return f"{n:.1f}"
    return f"{n:,}"
```

## Section

<!-- Body of the post -->

## Conclusion

<!-- Key takeaways -->

---

*Data current as of `{python} stats['data_current_as_of']`.*
